data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-04-03T00:52:39.785Z</current_datetime>

I'm migrating a .NET 10 web API to .NET 11. The app uses EF Core with the Azure Cosmos DB provider.
We have a repository pattern where some methods use synchronous LINQ:

```csharp
public class ProductRepository
{
    private readonly CosmosDbContext _context;

    public List<Product> GetAll()
    {
        return _context.Products.ToList();
    }

    public Product GetById(int id)
    {
        return _context.Products.First(p => p.Id == id);
    }

    public int GetCount()
    {
        return _context.Products.Count();
    }

    public void Save(Product product)
    {
        _context.Products.Add(product);
        _context.SaveChanges();
    }
}
```

In .NET 10 we had the sync-over-async opt-in configured. What breaks in .NET 11?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about what breaks when migrating from .NET 10 to .NET 11 with EF Core using the Azure Cosmos DB provider, specifically around synchronous LINQ operations.

This is a knowledge question about EF Core and the Cosmos DB provider. Let me answer based on what I know.

In EF Core, the Azure Cosmos DB provider has been moving toward async-only APIs. In .NET 10 (EF Core 10), synchronous operations on the Cosmos DB provider were deprecated but could still work with an opt-in configuration. In .NET 11 (EF Core 11), the sync-over-async support would be fully removed.
 
Actually, I need to reconsider the timeline here. Since we're in April 2026, .NET 10 launches in November 2025 and .NET 11 in November 2026. The Cosmos DB provider has been async-only at its core since EF Core 9, and synchronous methods like `ToList()`, `First()`, `Count()`, and `SaveChanges()` were already triggering warnings because they rely on sync-over-async wrapping internally. The Cosmos DB SDK itself is fundamentally async, so there's no true synchronous path available.

In .NET 10, they introduced a temporary opt-in mechanism to allow sync-over-async as a workaround, but by .NET 11, that opt-in is completely removed. So any code still using synchronous LINQ operations or `SaveChanges()` will throw `InvalidOperationException` with no fallback—the only solution is migrating to async/await patterns.

I should note that .NET 11 hasn't shipped yet, so I'm extrapolating from the published roadmap, but the direction is clear: the Cosmos provider is moving away from synchronous database operations entirely.

The configuration method to enable sync operations in earlier versions would be something like calling `EnableSyncOverAsync()` on the Cosmos options builder, but that escape hatch is being removed in the version we're discussing.
